---
title: "The Discretionary Power of Parole" 
format:
  html:
    css: styles.css
    embed-resources: TRUE
    code-fold: true
    page-layout: full
    fig_caption: yes
    toc: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev = "ragg_png"
)

source("R/config.R")

parole_elig_txt <- "Parole eligibility is a critical milestone in the criminal justice system, marking the point at which incarcerated individuals become eligible for conditional release. This eligibility is determined by several factors including the nature of the crime, the length of the sentence, behavior while incarcerated, and state-specific guidelines.<br>"

# Load prepared data
load(file = paste0(config$sp_data_path, "/data/analysis/app/map_percent.rds"))
load(file = paste0(config$sp_data_path, "/data/analysis/app/parole_eligibility_table.rds"))

num_people_current <- sum(parole_eligibility_table$current_count, na.rm = TRUE) 
```

<div id="body-section">
State leaders across the United States are grappling with multiple public safety challenges, including shortages in the correctional workforce, limited access to behavioral health services, and the ongoing task of supporting successful reentry for individuals returning from incarceration. Parole release discretion plays a pivotal role in addressing these challenges within 34 state justice systems.

In 2020, there were 88,661 people in prison past their parole eligibility year, highlighting the critical role that parole boards play in determining the timing of release. These delays underscore the importance of timely and appropriate parole decisions, which are crucial for managing prison capacity and ensuring that correctional systems can operate effectively amid staffing and resource constraints.  

```{r}
# Filter the dataframe where current_count is not NA and abolished_parole is "No"
filtered_df <- parole_eligibility_table[!is.na(parole_eligibility_table$current_count) & parole_eligibility_table$abolished_discretionary_parole == "No", ]

# Calculate the total current_count for this filtered data
total_current_count <- sum(filtered_df$current_count, na.rm = TRUE)

# Calculate the national percentage based on filtered_total_pop
national_percentage <- paste0(round(total_current_count / sum(filtered_df$filtered_total_pop, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 0), "%")

div(
  id = "grid-container",
  style = "
    display: grid; 
    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); 
    grid-template-rows: auto 1fr; 
    grid-row-gap: 0px; 
    grid-column-gap: 20px; 
    align-items: end;
  ",
  div(id = "national-highlight", formattable::comma(num_people_current, digits = 0)),
  img(src = "img/pe_1_in_3.png", style = "margin: 0 auto; max-width: 50%; height: auto; text-align: center;"),
  div(id = "national-highlight", "34"),
  div(id = "bold-text", HTML("In Prison Past Their<br>Parole Eligibility Year<br><br>")),
  div(id = "bold-text", HTML("1 in 3 Incarcerated People Are In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year")),
  div(id = "bold-text", HTML("States With<br>Discretionary Parole<br><br>"))
)
# div(
#   id = "grid-container",
#   style = "display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); grid-row-gap: 0px; grid-column-gap: 20px;",
#   div(id = "national-highlight", formattable::comma(num_people_current, digits = 0)),
#   # div(id = "national-highlight", national_percentage),
#   img(src = "img/pe_1_in_3.png", style = "margin: 0 auto; max-width: 50%; height: auto; text-align: center;"),
#   div(id = "national-highlight", "34"),
#   div(id = "bold-text", HTML("In Prison Past Their<br>Parole Eligibility Year")),
#   div(id = "bold-text", HTML("1 in 3 People Are In Prison Past<br>Their Parole Eligibility Year")),
#   div(id = "bold-text", HTML("States With<br>Discretionary Parole"))
# )
```
</div>
<br>
<div class="divider"></div>
<br>
<div id="map-section">
```{r}
map_percent |> 
    hc_size(height = 690) |> 
    hc_exporting(enabled = TRUE,
                 buttons = list(contextButton = list(align = 'right',
                                                     verticalAlign = 'top',
                                                     y = 0,
                                                     x = -5))) |> 
    hc_chart(marginTop = 0, marginBottom = -50, height = 740)
```
</div>

<a href="https://bjs.ojp.gov/data-collection/national-corrections-reporting-program-ncrp" style="font-size: 0.7em; color: gray;">About this data</a>

<div id="body-section">
<div class="divider"></div>

## The Power of Parole Boards

Despite the critical role they play, parole boards are often composed of a small number of individuals. In many states, just 5-10 parole board members are responsible for decisions affecting thousands of people awaiting parole.<br><br>Parole board members must balance a range of factors, including public safety, the rehabilitation progress of incarcerated people, and the perspectives of victims and their families. These boards often work with limited resources and under significant public and political pressure.

VISUALIZATION?

```{r}
# <div id="grid-container" style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 0px;">
#   <p><br>
#     Despite the critical role they play, parole boards are often composed of a small number of individuals. In many states, just 5-10 parole board members are responsible for decisions affecting thousands of people awaiting parole.<br><br>Parole board members must balance a range of factors, including public safety, the rehabilitation progress of incarcerated people, and the perspectives of victims and their families. These boards often work with limited resources and under significant public and political pressure.
#   </p>
#   ![](square_dot_parole_graphic.png)
# </div>
```



<div class="divider"></div>
## Parole Eligibility by State

This table provides a comprehensive overview of state-level prison populations*, highlighting the percentage and number of people in prison past their parole eligibility year, alongside details about whether the state has abolished discretionary parole and the number of parole board members.

```{r}
# Custom function to create data bars with NA handling
data_bars_custom <- function(value) {
  if (is.na(value)) {
    return("-")
  }
  percentage <- percent(value)
  bar <- div(style = list(
    background = "teal",
    width = percentage,
    height = "10px"
  ))
  div(style = list(display = "flex", alignItems = "center"),
      bar, div(style = list(marginLeft = "8px"), percentage))
}

style_cells <- function(value, color) {
  tags$span(style = paste("color:", color, ";"), value)
}

parole_eligibility_table$filtered_total_pop <- as.numeric(parole_eligibility_table$filtered_total_pop)

# https://kcuilla.github.io/reactablefmtr/articles/data_bars.html
reactable(
  parole_eligibility_table,
  pagination = FALSE,
  searchable = TRUE,
  defaultSortOrder = "desc",
  defaultSorted = "current_perc",
  defaultColDef = colDef(
    headerStyle = list(fontWeight = "normal"),
    sortable = TRUE,
    na = "-" 
  ),
  columns = list(
    state = colDef(name = "State", 
                   align = "left", 
                   width = 175,
                    cell = function(value) {
        url <- paste0("https://avparoleproject.netlify.app/state_report_", tolower(gsub(" ", "_", value)))
        tags$a(href = url, target = "_blank", style = "color:black;", value)
      }),
    current_perc = colDef(
      name = "Pct. of People in Prison",
      align = "left",
      width = 150,
      cell = data_bars_custom
    ),
    current_count = colDef(name = "Number of People", 
                           align = "right",
                           format = colFormat(separators = TRUE)),
    filtered_total_pop = colDef(name = "Prison Population*", 
                                align = "right",
                                format = colFormat(separators = TRUE)                           ),
    abolished_discretionary_parole = colDef(name = "Abolished Discretionary Parole", 
                                            align = "right"
                                            ),
    parole_board_members = colDef(name = "Parole Board Members", 
                                  align = "right"
                                  ),
    ratio = colDef(show = F, name = "Ratio", 
                   align = "center")
  ),
  columnGroups = list(
    colGroup(name = "In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year", columns = c("current_perc", "current_count"))
  )
)


```

<div id="footnotes-text"><br>*Includes people in prison for new crimes and with original sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. Does not include people in prison for parole revocations or sentence lengths less than one year or more than 25 years.<br></div>

<a href="https://bjs.ojp.gov/data-collection/national-corrections-reporting-program-ncrp" style="font-size: 0.7em; color: gray;">About This Data</a>
<a href="" style="font-size: 0.7em; color: gray;">Download Table</a>



<br>
<div class="divider"></div>
## Disparities 

Here we can show national disparities in incarceration rates and release after parole eligibility.   

Would you prefer this information to be displayed in this section or on the landing page? Should the policy paper be linked here?
["Learn More About Disparities Here."]

<br>
<div class="divider"></div>
## Economic Impact of Parole

Is it of interest to show costs? For instance, we could show potential cost savings, such as savings from timely release after parole eligibility. How much did it cost states on average to incarcerate people past their parole eligibility date?

Would you prefer this information to be displayed in this section or on the landing page?



<br>


</div>
<br>
